title: Production managers and directors in manufacturing
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the work activities and resources necessary for manufacturing products in accordance with cost, quality, and quantity specifications. Plan, direct, or coordinate quality assurance programs. Formulate quality control policies and control quality of laboratory and production efforts. Manage operations at geothermal power generation facilities. Maintain and monitor geothermal plant equipment for efficient and safe plant operations. (Based on 12 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Direct or coordinate production, processing, distribution, or marketing activities of industrial organizations, including biofuels and geothermal power generation facilities. Conduct cost, material, and efficiency studies, manage budgets, and supervise operations, maintenance, and quality assurance activities. Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements, communicate quality control information, and oversee testing activities throughout production cycles.

Monitor and manage operations at biofuels and geothermal power generation facilities. Shut down and restart plants or equipment in emergency situations, review logs and performance specifications, adjust equipment controls, and ensure proper operation through real-time data monitoring. Conduct field inspections, and prepare reports on operations and maintenance status.

Develop and implement safety programs and training for employees in geothermal and biofuels plants, ensuring compliance with safety, environmental, and operational standards and regulations. Identify opportunities for improving plant operations and maintain records associated with operations and maintenance.

Oversee and manage budgets for biofuels and geothermal operations, ensuring efficient use of materials, labor, and equipment. Review and approve proposals for the acquisition or repair of processing equipment, and communicate with vendors and contractors regarding quality control and operational needs.

Establish quality control policies and monitor the performance of quality control systems in biofuels and geothermal operations. Conduct inspections and testing of production processes, document testing procedures, and oversee training in quality control and analytical methods.

Collaborate with technical and supervisory personnel, marketing departments, and other stakeholders to ensure alignment between production activities and client requirements. Analyze power production trends to identify opportunities for operational improvements and enforce compliance with operational standards.

Plan and schedule plant activities, such as fuel deliveries and maintenance, and supervise employees in geothermal and biofuels facilities to ensure effective operation. Review performance logs and data to ensure production levels are met and implement corrective measures when necessary.

Perform or direct the performance of preventative maintenance on geothermal and biofuels plant equipment, ensuring adherence to safety guidelines and regulatory standards. Identify and evaluate operational inefficiencies and develop management and operational procedures as required.

Monitor equipment and instrumentation within biofuels and geothermal plants for safety and efficiency, conducting regular inspections and maintaining operational logs. Provide technical direction in the setup and commissioning of necessary systems and machinery.

Identify critical points in manufacturing and production processes, specifying sampling procedures, and directly tracking defects and quality control data. Use this information to inform management decisions and enhance operational efficiency.
Research and implement projects to improve efficiency, economy, and effectiveness of plant operations while ensuring compliance with environmental standards during the operational processes.

Review processing schedules or production orders to make decisions concerning inventory requirements, staffing requirements, work procedures, or duty assignments, considering budgetary limitations and time constraints.
Review operations and confer with technical or administrative staff to resolve production or processing problems.
Hire, train, evaluate, or discharge staff or resolve personnel grievances.
Initiate or coordinate inventory or cost control programs.
Prepare and maintain production reports or personnel records.
Set and monitor product standards, examining samples of raw products or directing testing during processing, to ensure finished products are of prescribed quality.
Develop or implement production tracking or quality control systems, analyzing production, quality control, maintenance, or other operational reports to detect production problems.
Review plans and confer with research or support staff to develop new products or processes.
Coordinate or recommend procedures for facility or equipment maintenance or modification, including the replacement of machines.
Maintain current knowledge of the quality control field, relying on current literature pertaining to materials use, technological advances, or statistical studies.
Negotiate materials prices with suppliers.
Monitor development of new products to help identify possible problems for mass production and participate in the development of product specifications.
Formulate basic layout design or presentation approach and specify material details.
Supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in film editing, assembling, and recording activities.
Stop production if serious product defects are present and review and approve quality plans submitted by contractors.
Generate and maintain quality control operating budgets and evaluate new testing and sampling methodologies or technologies to determine usefulness.

Collect and analyze production samples to evaluate quality, verify that raw materials and finished products meet established testing and inspection standards, and review quality documentation necessary for regulatory submissions and inspections.

Review and update standard operating procedures or quality assurance manuals and produce reports regarding nonconformance of products or processes, daily production quality, root cause analyses, or quality trends.
Identify quality problems or areas for improvement and recommend solutions, as well as instruct vendors or contractors on quality guidelines, testing procedures, or ways to eliminate deficiencies.
Coordinate the selection and implementation of quality control equipment, such as inspection gauges, and conduct audits and inspections of subcontractor facilities including external laboratories.
